worrying.en: causing you to feel worried or anxious

ex.en: The rising number of cases is worrying.
ex.ko: 증가하는 확진자 수가 걱정스럽다.

ex.en: Her condition worsened overnight.
ex.ko: 그녀의 상태는 밤사이 악화되었다.

worsen.en: make a bad situation even worse

ex.en: The drought worsened the food shortage.
ex.ko: 가뭄이 식량 부족을 악화시켰다.

worship.en: a religious ceremony in which people show love and respect for a god

ex.en: Sunday worship begins at ten o'clock.
ex.ko: 일요일 예배는 10시에 시작한다.

worship.en: pray to and show love and respect for a god

ex.en: They gather every week to worship together.
ex.ko: 그들은 매주 함께 예배드리기 위해 모인다.

worship.en: admire and love someone so much that you treat them as perfect

ex.en: He worshipped his older brother as a child.
ex.ko: 그는 어릴 때 형을 우상처럼 떠받들었다.

worthless.en: having no real value or usefulness

ex.en: The old coins turned out to be worthless.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 동전들은 알고 보니 가치가 없었다.

worthless.en: (of a person) deserving no respect because of bad behavior or qualities

ex.en: His constant criticism made her feel worthless.
ex.ko: 그의 끊임없는 비난은 그녀를 스스로 쓸모없다고 느끼게 만들었다.

wreck.en: a ship that has been destroyed or badly damaged at sea

ex.en: Divers explored the wreck of the sunken ship.
ex.ko: 잠수부들은 침몰한 배의 난파선을 탐사했다.

wreck.en: a vehicle that has been badly damaged in a serious accident

ex.en: The car was a total wreck after the crash.
ex.ko: 그 차는 충돌 후 완전히 부서진 잔해가 되었다.

wreck.en: a person who is in very bad physical or mental condition

ex.en: After the divorce, he was an emotional wreck.
ex.ko: 이혼 후 그는 정서적으로 만신창이가 되었다.

ex.en: The storm wrecked several boats in the harbor.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍은 항구에 있던 여러 배를 파괴했다.

wrongly.en: in a way that is unfair or unjust

ex.en: She was wrongly blamed for the mistake.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 실수에 대해 부당하게 비난받았다.

wrongly.en: in an incorrect or mistaken way

ex.en: His name was wrongly spelled on the certificate.
ex.ko: 그의 이름이 증명서에 잘못 표기되었다.

x-ray.en: a photograph of the inside of your body made using a special kind of radiation

ex.en: The doctor took an X-ray of her broken arm.
ex.ko: 의사는 그녀의 부러진 팔을 엑스레이로 찍었다.

x-ray.en: examine part of someone's body by taking an X-ray photograph of it

ex.en: They X-rayed his chest to check for pneumonia.
ex.ko: 그들은 폐렴 여부를 확인하려고 그의 가슴을 엑스레이로 찍었다.

x-ray.en: a form of radiation that can pass through solid objects and is used to see inside things

ex.en: X-rays can pass through skin but not bone.
ex.ko: 엑스선은 피부는 통과하지만 뼈는 통과하지 못한다.

yacht.en: a boat with sails or an engine, used for racing or pleasure trips

ex.en: They spent the weekend sailing on a yacht.
ex.ko: 그들은 요트를 타고 주말을 보냈다.

yawn.cue: 졸리거나 지루할 때 입을 벌리는 행동
yawn.en: an act of opening your mouth wide and breathing in deeply, usually when tired or bored

ex.en: She let out a big yawn during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중에 크게 하품을 했다.

yawn.en: open your mouth wide and breathe in deeply because you are tired or bored

ex.en: He yawned loudly in the middle of the lecture.
ex.ko: 그는 강의 중간에 크게 하품을 했다.

yawn.en: (of a gap or opening) be wide open, in a way that seems ready to swallow something

ex.en: A dark chasm yawned before them.
ex.ko: 어두운 협곡이 그들 앞에 쩍 벌어져 있었다.

yearn.en: want something very much, especially something that is hard to have

ex.en: She yearned for a quieter life in the countryside.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시골에서의 더 조용한 삶을 갈망했다.

yearn.en: feel a strong sense of longing for someone or something that is not present

ex.en: He yearned for his hometown after years abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 몇 년간 해외에 있으면서 고향을 사무치게 그리워했다.

yield.en: the amount of a crop or product that is produced

ex.en: Farmers reported a higher crop yield this year.
ex.ko: 농부들은 올해 더 높은 수확량을 보고했다.

yield.en: produce or provide a result, product, or profit

ex.en: The experiment yielded surprising results.
ex.ko: 그 실험은 놀라운 결과를 산출했다.

yield.en: the profit or income produced by an investment, shown as a percentage

ex.en: The bond offers a yield of five percent.
ex.ko: 그 채권은 5퍼센트의 수익률을 제공한다.

yield.en: stop resisting and agree to what someone wants

ex.en: The government refused to yield to the protesters' demands.
ex.ko: 정부는 시위대의 요구에 굴복하기를 거부했다.

yield.en: allow another vehicle to go first at a road junction

ex.en: Drivers must yield to pedestrians at the crosswalk.
ex.ko: 운전자는 횡단보도에서 보행자에게 양보해야 한다.

youthful.en: looking or seeming young and full of energy

ex.en: Despite being sixty, she has a youthful energy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 예순 살임에도 젊음이 넘치는 활기를 지녔다.

ex.en: This cake is so yummy!
ex.ko: 이 케이크 정말 맛있다!

zebra.cue: 검은색과 흰색 줄무늬가 있는 아프리카 동물
zebra.en: an African wild animal like a horse, with black and white stripes

ex.en: Zebras live in large herds on the African plains.
ex.ko: 얼룩말은 아프리카 초원에서 큰 무리를 지어 산다.

zip.en: a fastener with two rows of teeth that lock together, used to close clothes or bags

ex.en: Her jacket has a broken zip.
ex.ko: 그녀의 재킷은 지퍼가 고장 났다.

ex.en: He zipped up his coat before going outside.
ex.ko: 그는 밖에 나가기 전에 코트 지퍼를 잠갔다.

ex.en: The new coach brought some zip to the team.
ex.ko: 새 코치는 팀에 활기를 불어넣었다.

ex.en: Cars zipped past on the highway.
ex.ko: 차들이 고속도로 위를 쌩 하고 지나갔다.

zoom.en: a sudden, steep rise, especially upward into the air

ex.en: The small plane pulled into a steep zoom to avoid the hill.
ex.ko: 그 소형 비행기는 언덕을 피하려고 가파르게 급상승했다.

zoom.en: make an image on a camera or screen look bigger or closer, or smaller and farther away

ex.en: She zoomed in on his face in the photo.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사진 속 그의 얼굴을 확대했다.

zoom.en: move very fast, especially with a loud noise

ex.en: The motorcycle zoomed past us on the highway.
ex.ko: 그 오토바이가 고속도로에서 우리 옆을 쌩 하고 지나갔다.
